fundamentals.name: Bloom Energy Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. It offers Bloom Energy Server, an energy server platform to convert fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels, into electricity through a non-combustion electrochemical process. The company also provides Bloom Electrolyzer for producing hydrogen. It sells its products through direct and indirect sales channels to utilities, data centers, retail, healthcare, education, telecom, manufacturing, and other industries. The company was formerly known as Ion America Corp. and changed its name to Bloom Energy Corporation in 2006. Bloom Energy Corporation was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with strong revenue expansion but high valuation and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 242.4%; next earnings date is 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 242.4% — strong operational performance.
Analyst growth estimates are very high for current quarter (286.1%) and FY (179.8%).
Gross margin 30.1% and operating margin 9.6% indicate healthy core business operations.
Current ratio 5.03 and quick ratio 3.98 show excellent short-term liquidity.
negatives: Forward P/E of 68.62 and Price/Sales of 34.45 indicate a very high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 311.48 is very high, suggesting significant leverage.
Profit margin of 0.2% and Return on equity of 1.3% are very low.
Total debt ($2.95B) is higher than total cash ($2.49B).

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) has been highlighted as a top energy stock pick across multiple recent articles, with one report noting a significant $2.6 billion agreement with Nebius. Jim Cramer has also enthusiastically endorsed the stock, calling it a "rocket ship." While the company's latest reported quarter showed a substantial EPS beat, the forward P/E remains high, suggesting that significant future growth is already priced in.
